author: @bobweiram6321
text: I would use PVC cement instead of CLA glue. PVC welds them together, whereas the CLA is more likely to crack from vibration and impact.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: good idea mate I used CA because that is what is used here in the UK building industry for doors, windows and roofline - so lots of vibration there and a 20 year guarantee

author: @lnwolf41
text: Interesting, you call it an "H" beam, in the U.S. we call it an "I" beam.  Over here, they take a 10" widex 12' long piece of plywood, and glue 2  2"x2" strip of wood to make a beam for spanning a house foundation

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I have seen that mate - it is - to be honest - awesome

replies.author: @kezzatries
replies.text: Same here in Oz and NZ , I beam

author: @Kiyarose3999
text: I’ve been thinking about saying this for a while but didn’t want to sound/be negative, but you have now started another project. When you already have 4 half completed projects, an electric Scooter, a Lawnmower-motor vehicle, two VAWT’s one of which you gave the heading ‘’1Kw Wind Gene’’( or words to that effect). Will any of them reappear in the near future?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: It's not negative mate - some things take time, some things I consider complete - like the VAWTs for example - for me they're done and dusted - other stuff takes more money that I don't have - if I find the right motor for free or have a spare few hundred - I will finish them

author: @sylvanenergy
text: You created a nice shelf space for batteries. Always educational entertainment Old Boy!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @zacsmith3903
text: Robert love your videos. Could you tell me what is the thickness of the builders board you use? I could not understand what you said the board was made of. I am trying to find the equivalent in Australia.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: 9mm it's a foam core board - here it is a uPVC outer but not sure what the inner foam is looks like polyurethane to me

author: @MrAnderson4509
text:
Great project and material exploration, never really explored Sintra board, the only time I used it was to check on CNC for making a really large chain wheel for the three wheel electric race car we built👍
would it be ok for a vid link? the Quality is poor and dated, or maybe a picture of the project , I still feel bad about not just posting a schematic for that motor amplifier

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: of course it would mate - post away - it won't go up immediately I will have to clear it - but I will do

author: @randycrowe4978
text: That is amazing.  I was gifted a home made trike 12 years ago and still use it in the summer...  but, it is made from steel exhaust pipes and weighs well over a hundred pounds.  I can only use it on flat areas because of the extra weight...  and mine.   Your trike is going to be incredible.   Always love your videos.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is certainly going to be light mate

author: @McRootbeer
text: Awesome!  Please go all the way with this one! It would be so cool to see how a 100% diy e- vehicle would do.  Diy frame (anybody got thoughts on regular trike configuration vs reverse trike? Pros? Cons? Cool factor?). Diy motor (design parameters, cost vs performance, diy motor performance vs off the shelf motor performance, potential upgrades to diy motor down the line). And especially Diy battery pack w/ controller.  Still anticipating the vid on the super ultra type 3 Daniel cell.  Or a big paper battery with iron copper baghdad battery inks.  So excited for what's next!!  Please do some videos on the planning process.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I never do vid on the planning - I think you are right - I should

author: @TecArtScien
text: I don't understand the project, try to post a picture of the finished project to give is a perspective

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: you are right I should have done that - cheers mate

author: @locouk
text:
I think rear wheel steering is going to be very unstable, it’ll be interestingly see the result. 
  It’s a pity these videos can’t be 15 minutes long!

replies.author: @johnmcfadden9336
replies.text:
You could make it nearly 20 mins if you play it at 1 quarter speed 😃
I’m sure some people would appreciate a closer look at some of the details that rob skips over which would include me at times but short and sweet are good to, I think rob is trying to avoiding wasting our time with unnecessary details and we get one a day

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh for sure mate - it is likely to slide all over

replies.author: @locouk
replies.text:
@johnmcfadden9336 
  For sure, editing can be really time consuming too. Longer videos would mean less watching time on average, I mean shorter videos, people are less likely to skip though the videos to get to the bit they want to see.

author: @dgpreston5593
text: Curious to see where this goes.  Have you ever tried scoring or cutting a lengthwise channel in the board and then folding along the hinge?  It would make an L instead of an H, but require fewer operations.  Just a random suggestion on technique.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: never tried it mate - my thought is it might be less operations but a bit trickier than a straight cut - still - nice suggestion if you fancy giving it a go
